title: [Feature]: Improve Oracle duplicate handling controls and visibility
problem: Oracle duplicate and conflict metrics exist, but operators may need clearer control over which columns are updated during merge operations and what can be observed when a message matches an existing durable row. Without clearer controls, teams may either over-update stored records or lack enough visibility to understand duplicate redelivery behavior.
proposal: Add explicit Oracle merge update controls, document insert-versus-match visibility, and expose safe duplicate outcome metrics where Oracle execution metadata supports it reliably. The feature should avoid fragile driver assumptions and should preserve current idempotent defaults.
users: Oracle sink operators who use merge mode in high-volume or audited environments and need predictable behavior when redelivered messages match existing durable rows.
delivery_semantics: Duplicate detection must remain a sink-level durable-write concern. A duplicate handled successfully in idempotent mode may return success only after the Oracle transaction has committed. Metrics and visibility must never decide ACK behavior.
security: Metrics and logs must not expose table names, constraint names, subjects, message IDs, classifications, labels, or payloads by default. SQL identifiers must remain allow-list validated and values must remain bound.
acceptance: Merge update behavior can be configured with clear defaults.
Duplicate and conflict visibility is documented with Oracle limitations.
Metrics remain low-cardinality and readable through the existing metrics CLI.
Tests cover duplicate, conflict, merge-match, and insert paths where practical.
Existing Oracle configurations continue to work without breaking changes.
tests: Add Oracle SQL generation tests, mapping tests, duplicate metric tests, and optional integration coverage for merge-match behavior. Run scripts/check-sinks.sh and scripts/check.sh.
documentation: Update docs/oracle-sink.md, docs/metrics.md, docs/idempotency.md, docs/configuration.md, ROADMAP.md, and CHANGELOG.md.
closeout: Close after release publication. The close-out note must include the release tag, controls added, observable outcomes, tests run, and any Oracle metadata limitations.
